content:
- Completed `bug-verify-http-allows-warning-only-insecure-listener-modes` end to end and pushed commit `4c1250b` to `master`.
- Enforced an HTTPS+mTLS-only verify-service listener contract in config, removed the warning-only insecure path and plaintext runtime branch, rewrote verify-service fixtures/tests, updated the runner verify-image harness to use secure listener config over HTTPS+mTLS, and fixed the latent runtime bug by passing the configured server cert/key to `ListenAndServeTLS`.
- Validation passed: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`; also ran focused Go tests and the two verify-image regression tests while fixing the default-lane failure. Next: stop immediately and let Ralph pick the next task.

- Read prior progress: last completed task was the ultra-high bug `bug-verify-http-allows-warning-only-insecure-listener-modes`, with check/lint/test all green.
- Read the task summaries and the `list-active-tasks` skill instructions. Current active backlog includes one remaining ultra-high failing bug: `bug-verify-http-exposes-job-results-and-metrics-without-auth`.
- Next I am refreshing the Ralph summary and checking the repo layout to confirm the actual stack and whether any blocker metadata changes the pick.

- Refreshed the Ralph task summaries and re-read the choose-task rules.
- Confirmed the real repo architecture from the codebase: Rust workspace (`Cargo.toml`, `crates/runner`, `crates/source-bootstrap`) plus a Go verify-service implementation under `cockroachdb_molt/molt/verifyservice`, with `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` all mapped to Cargo commands.
- Checked active task files for blocker tags. All active failing bugs are unblocked; the only blocked active task is `story-22a-runner-metrics/02`, blocked by `story-22a-runner-metrics/01`.
- Chosen next task is the unblocked ultra-high failing bug `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-http-exposes-job-results-and-metrics-without-auth.md`, which outranks every other active task under the mandatory bug-first and priority rules.
- Next after this context exits: leave `.ralph/current_task.txt` pointing at that bug and stop immediately.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Active task is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-http-exposes-job-results-and-metrics-without-auth.md`; next I am reading the task details and locating the verify-service auth/result/metrics code paths to decide whether to plan or execute.

- The task file is not in `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn is the planning pass.
- Verified the current boundary problem: `Service.Handler()` directly exposes `GET /jobs/{job_id}` and `GET /metrics`, and `metrics.go` emits database/table/job labels that the bug calls sensitive.
- Next I am reading the exact job response assembly and nearby tests so I can write a concrete TDD + boundary-improvement plan file, mark it `TO BE VERIFIED`, and stop.

- Before writing the plan, I checked whether verify-service already has an application-auth seam or only transport-level mTLS.
- This matters because the cleanest boundary fix may be to remove sensitive read endpoints from the public handler entirely instead of inventing a second auth layer.

- Read the active bug task, the relevant `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skills, and the verify-service read-surface code/tests.
- Confirmed the listener already enforces HTTPS+mTLS; the remaining bug is that public `GET /jobs/{job_id}` and `GET /metrics` still expose sensitive verification detail after connection admission.
- Wrote the execution-ready plan at `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-http-exposes-job-results-and-metrics-without-auth_plans/2026-04-19-verify-http-read-surface-hardening-plan.md` and ended it with `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Chosen design: shrink the public read contract instead of inventing a new app-auth subsystem, and use the task as a boundary cleanup to remove or privatize leaky result/metrics shapes.
- Next after quitting immediately: execute the plan with strict one-test-at-a-time TDD, then run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, and only touch `make test-long` if the long-lane verify-image harness contract gets changed.

- Reloaded Ralph progress, the active task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed this bug already has an execution-ready plan and the next step is to execute that plan in strict vertical TDD.
- Next I am reading the task file and the existing plan, then I will inspect the verify-service read endpoints and their tests before making the first red test change.

- Read the verify-service handlers, progress snapshot code, metrics collector, and most of the HTTP integration tests.
- Confirmed the boundary smell from the plan is real: public job/metrics rendering is coupled directly to internal progress accumulation, and `/metrics` leaks `job_id`, database names, schema names, table names, mismatch kinds, and runtime error counts.
- Next I am finishing the test/code read, then I will add the first red test for the reduced `GET /jobs/{job_id}` contract before making the minimal code change to pass it.

- Added the first tracer-bullet red test in `cockroachdb_molt/molt/verifyservice/http_test.go` for a completed job returning only `job_id` and `status`, with no leaked table/report text.
- The first `go test` invocation failed only because I ran it from the repo root; the Go module root is `cockroachdb_molt/molt`.
- Next I am rerunning the focused test from the Go module root, then I will make the minimal handler change that removes the leaky job response fields.

- Completed the first red-green slice for `GET /jobs/{job_id}`: added a red integration test for a status-only completed-job response, changed the handler to return only `job_id` and `status`, and moved detailed result DTOs behind `progress.go` so they are no longer part of the handler boundary.
- The focused tracer-bullet test now passes under `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto`.
- Next I am running the full `verifyservice` package to surface the next red failures, then I will remove or rewrite the old tests and dead public result-shape assumptions one slice at a time.

- Added the metrics tracer-bullet test expecting only coarse lifecycle metrics and confirmed it fails because `/metrics` still exposes `job_id`, database, schema, table, mismatch-kind, and error-count detail.
- The cleanest fix is to replace the collector with lifecycle-only gauges/counters and remove the now-unused source/target database-name plumbing from `Service`.
- Next I am editing `metrics.go` and `service.go` to publish only coarse status metrics, then I will rerun the focused metrics test before updating the old metrics assertions.

- Replaced the leaked `/metrics` families with coarse lifecycle metrics only, deleted the no-longer-justified verify-service progress accumulator file, and kept the package green.
- Found that default `cargo test --workspace` includes non-ignored verify-image tests, so I moved the Rust verify-image audit boundary off the public HTTP `result` payload and onto structured verify container logs while keeping the public job response lifecycle-only.
- Next I am running the Rust contract test for the new typed integrity boundary, then I will run the required repo-wide `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` lanes.

- `make check` passed after restoring the existing runtime-shape/source-command audit fields and variants that the E2E harness expects.
- The verify correctness boundary remains moved off the public HTTP result payload and onto structured verify container logs.
- Next I am running `make lint` and then `make test`; if the repo-wide test lane exposes any runtime mismatch in the new log-backed audit, I will fix it before finishing the task.

- Repo-wide validation passed: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` are all green after moving verify correctness assertions onto structured verify-image logs and shrinking the Go verify-service public surface to lifecycle-only status plus coarse lifecycle metrics.
- Next I am updating the task file metadata and acceptance boxes, then I will run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, commit all tracked changes including `.ralph`, and push.
- If context ended right now, the work itself is done and only completion bookkeeping remains.
